headline: Tradr's 2X Short Leveraged ETFs on AAOI and ORCL Begin Trading
summary: Tradr ETFs, a provider of ETFs designed for sophisticated investors and professional traders, today launched two new leveraged ETFs on the stocks of Applied Optoelectronics and Oracle Corporation. The Cboe-listed funds seek to deliver two times short (-200%) the daily performance of their underlying target stocks.

The Cboe-listed funds seek to deliver two times short (-200%) the daily performance of their underlying target stocks.

headline: Gridiron Capital Partners with Centroid
summary: Gridiron Capital, LLC ("Gridiron Capital" or "Gridiron"), an investment firm focused on partnering with founders, entrepreneurs, and management teams, is pleased to announce an investment in Centroid (the "Company"), a premier Oracle Cloud Infrastructure ("OCI") service provider headquartered in Troy, Michigan.

content.title: Gridiron Capital Partners with Centroid
content.summary: Gridiron Capital, LLC ("Gridiron Capital" or "Gridiron"), an investment firm focused on partnering with founders, entrepreneurs, and management teams, is pleased to announce an investment in Centroid (the "Company"), a premier Oracle Cloud Infrastructure ("OCI") service provider headquartered in Troy, Michigan.

headline: Oracle shares gain after report it leads race for Japanese government cloud contract (ORCL)
summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Monday after a report suggested the company is leading the competition to provide cloud infrastructure services to the Japanese government. Oracle reportedly ahead in bidding processAccording to a Financial Times report citing seven people familiar with the discussions, Oracle is currently ahead of Amazon Web Services, Microsoft and Google in negotiations over the potential contract.

content.title: Oracle shares gain after report it leads race for Japanese government cloud contract (ORCL)
content.summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Monday after a report suggested the company is leading the competition to provide cloud infrastructure services to the Japanese government. Oracle reportedly ahead in bidding processAccording to a Financial Times report citing seven people familiar with the discussions, Oracle is currently ahead of Amazon Web Services, Microsoft and Google in negotiations over the potential contract.

headline: Connectbase and InterGlobix Announce Oracle's Dr. Sanjay Basu and NVIDIA's Pete Coticchia to Headline The Connected World LIVE! 2026 with Opening Keynote and Executive Fireside Chat

Ongoing investment in AI focused cloud offerings can support long run revenue expectations, while heavy data center spending and related profitability...
